mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate revenue growth expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of fiscal stimulus.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in household savings rates compresses real disposable income, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises >0.5% monthly while RSAFS declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <50% of periods with rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes where capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit growth, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.10 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing anticipated monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL crosses 260 during periods of accommodative central bank guidance, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all post-threshold windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which elevates consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when wage growth exceeds 5% annually, indicating inflationary dominance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding residential investment and construction activity, which stimulates upstream industrial demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >60% of periods with rising HOUST, or the signal loses predictive power when mortgage rates exceed 4.5%, indicating financing constraints override demand signals.
